check-cluster.template: Check the overall health of the Kubernetes cluster. Review all namespaces, look for failing pods, check PVC status, review resource usage, and summarize any issues found.
check-cluster.description: Health check for the entire cluster

inspect-logs.template: Use kubectl to get recent logs from {{namespace}}/{{pod}} and analyze them for errors, warnings, or issues. Summarize what you find.

review-chart.template: Review the Helm chart at {{chart_path}}. Check for security issues, best practices, and suggest improvements based on the homelab CLAUDE.md guidelines.
review-chart.description: Review a Helm chart for issues

check-argocd.template: Check all ArgoCD applications for sync status, health, and any sync failures. Report on what needs attention.

troubleshoot.template: Help me troubleshoot {{service}} in namespace {{namespace}}. Check pod status, logs, events, and service configuration. Provide a diagnosis and solution.

add-service.template: Help me add a new service to the homelab. Create a Helm chart for {{service_name}}, add ArgoCD application manifests, and follow all security and observability best practices from CLAUDE.md.
add-service.description: Scaffold a new service with all required manifests

review-security.template: Review {{resource}} for security issues. Check: non-root user, read-only filesystem, no privilege escalation, dropped capabilities, resource limits, network policies, and secret management.

optimize-resources.template:
Optimize Kubernetes resource requests and limits based on actual usage.

CONTEXT:
- Helm charts are in charts/**
- Environment-specific values are in overlays/{cluster-critical,prod,dev}/**
- Each overlay/<env>/<service>/ contains values.yaml with resource overrides

PROCESS:
1. Gather current resource usage data:
   - Run: kubectl top pods --all-namespaces --no-headers
   - Collect CPU and memory usage for all pods over the last 7 days if available
   - Note: Use current usage as baseline, apply 20% buffer for spikes

2. Analyze resource definitions:
   - Search for all values.yaml files in charts/** and overlays/**
   - Extract current resource requests and limits
   - Map pods to their source chart/overlay

3. Calculate optimizations:
   - For CPU: Set requests to P95 usage + 20% buffer
   - For memory: Set requests to P95 usage + 20% buffer
   - Set limits to 2x requests (allow bursting)
   - Flag over-provisioned resources (>3x actual usage)
   - Flag under-provisioned resources (<actual P95 usage)

4. Generate changes:
   - Update values.yaml files in appropriate locations:
     * For chart defaults: charts/<service>/values.yaml
     * For env-specific: overlays/<env>/<service>/values.yaml
   - Preserve existing structure and comments
   - Add comment explaining the optimization

5. Create PR:
   - Create a new branch: optimize-resources-$(date +%Y%m%d)
   - Commit changes with message:
     'chore: optimize resource requests based on actual usage

     - Analyzed resource usage across all namespaces
     - Updated requests to P95 + 20% buffer
     - Set limits to 2x requests for burst capacity
     - Estimated cost savings: [calculate based on changes]

     Generated with opencode /optimize-resources'
   - Push branch and create PR with summary table showing:
     * Service name
     * Current vs new requests
     * Actual usage
     * Estimated savings

REQUIREMENTS:
- Only modify resources.requests and resources.limits
- Maintain YAML formatting and comments
- Follow homelab patterns (security context, etc.)
- Include cost impact in PR description
- Do not modify resource limits for databases or stateful services (be conservative)

OUTPUT:
- Summary of changes
- PR URL
- Estimated monthly cost savings
optimize-resources.description: Optimize resource requests/limits based on actual usage and create PR

save-context.template:
Save the current session context to preserve work history and rationale.

Review the conversation history and git changes, then create a context document with:

## SESSION CONTEXT

### Completed Tasks
- List all tasks that were completed in this session
- Include specific files changed and what was modified
- Reference any commands run or PRs created

### Tasks To Do
- List remaining tasks or follow-up items
- Include any blockers or decisions needed
- Note any testing or validation required

### Rationale & Decisions
- Explain WHY changes were made (not just what)
- Document any architectural decisions
- Note any alternatives considered and why they were rejected
- Include relevant context for future maintainers

### References
- Links to relevant documentation
- Related issues or PRs
- SigNoz dashboards or logs if relevant

Save this as .session/context-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).md and commit it with message:
'docs: save session context

Preserve work context for future reference

Generated with opencode /save-context'

OUTPUT:
- Path to context file
- Summary of what was documented
save-context.description: Save session context with completed tasks, TODOs, and rationale

pre-pr.template:
Run comprehensive pre-PR checks before creating a pull request.

EXECUTE THE FOLLOWING STEPS IN ORDER:

1. FORMAT CODE:
   - Run: bazel run //tools/format:format
   - This formats Go, JavaScript, Python, Rust, Shell, Starlark files
   - Also updates apko locks and renders manifests
   - If formatting fails, stop and report errors
   - Commit any formatting changes with message: 'chore: format code'

2. BUILD ALL:
   - Run: bazel build //...
   - Build all targets in the repository
   - If any build fails, stop and report which targets failed
   - Include build timing and any warnings

3. TEST ALL:
   - Run: bazel test //...
   - Run all tests in the repository
   - If any tests fail, stop and report:
     * Which tests failed
     * Test output/logs
     * Suggestions for fixing
   - Include test coverage summary if available

4. PUSH IMAGES:
   - Identify which images need to be pushed based on changed files
   - For ttyd-session-manager changes:
     * Push backend: bazel run //charts/ttyd-session-manager/backend:image_push
     * Push ttyd-worker: bazel run //charts/ttyd-session-manager/backend:ttyd_worker_image_push
   - For other changed charts, push their images
   - Report image tags and registry URLs

5. VALIDATION:
   - Run: git status to show any uncommitted changes
   - Check for common issues:
     * Uncommitted files
     * Merge conflicts
     * Large files (>1MB)
   - Verify images were pushed successfully

6. SUMMARY:
   - Report overall status (PASS/FAIL)
   - List any warnings or issues found
   - Suggest next steps (create PR, fix issues, etc.)
   - Include timing for each step

IF ANY STEP FAILS:
- Stop execution immediately
- Report the failure clearly
- Provide actionable suggestions to fix
- Do NOT proceed to next steps

OUTPUT:
- Detailed status for each step
- Total time taken
- Ready for PR: YES/NO
- Next recommended action
pre-pr.description: Run format, build, test, and push images before creating PR
